content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN positive integers.

An integer XXX is said to be good if there exists indices iii and jjj (1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N1 \le i, j \le N1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N) such that Ai<XA_i < XAi‚Äč<X and X<AjX < A_jX<Aj‚Äč (i.e. there exists a smaller element and a larger element).

Find the number of good integers. It can be proven that the answer is always finite.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,...,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output the number of good integers.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : It can be shown that only the integers 222, 555, 333 and 444 are good.
For example, 222 is good because we can choose i=3i = 3i=3 and j=2j = 2j=2, Ai=1<2=XA_i = 1 < 2 = XAi‚Äč=1<2=X and Aj=3>2=XA_j = 3 > 2 = XAj‚Äč=3>2=X.
